title: astragalus cruciatus var. longicaulis
intro: Astragalus cruciatus var. longicaulis, also known as the Long-stemmed Cruciate Milk-vetch, is a plant species within the legume family, native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. Traditionally, the roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as immune-boosting and adaptogenic properties. The health benefits associated with Astragalus cruciatus var. longicaulis include improved immune function, reduced stress and fatigue, and enhanced overall well-being. Astragalus cruciatus var. longicaulis can be prepared into various herbal forms, including dried root powder, teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
